source_jobs.title: General Application
source_jobs.description:
JOB DESCRIPTION

Don’t see a direct role but want to chat with the Glimpse about opportunities? Feel free to reach out here!


IDEAL QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience working in highly cross-functional roles

 - Ability work 5 days a week in our NYC office

Why Work At Glimpse?

Glimpse is at an incredibly exciting inflection point - going from 0→100 customers in 8 months with strong product market fit. We have a high agency culture and are continuing to scale rapidly. We’re a default in person office culture. As an early hire, you’ll be part of a tight-knit team with an opportunity to make a massive impact.


BENEFITS

 - Competitive salary and equity

 - Relocation assistance to NYC

 - Comprehensive medical, vision and dental insurance plans offered

 - 401k plan offered

 - Unlimited PTO

source_jobs.title: Mid-Market Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About Glimpse

Glimpse is the leading AI platform for CPG brands — automating critical back-office workflows like deductions management, revenue recovery, and cash application. Since launching in April 2024, we've grown from 0 to 200+ customers, raised $52M from investors including a16z, 8VC and Y Combinator.

Our AI agents retrieve deduction data, validate charges, automate cash application, and dispute invalid claims — work that would take a full-time employee years to complete. For a $1B CPG brand, a single Glimpse agent reviewed 17,000 deductions in under 24 hours, identifying over $10M in recoverable revenue.

We're building the next-generation suite of services for consumer brands and are looking for exceptional people to help us scale.

About the Role

Glimpse is looking for a Mid-Market Account Executive(s) to join the team and own new business growth across our core market of consumer brands. Reporting to the VP of Sales, you'll be responsible for driving net new revenue by prospecting, qualifying, and closing high-quality deals with food, beverage, health, and beauty brands across the US. You'll be expected to meet and exceed quota by running a disciplined, full-cycle sales motion, and play a key role in scaling Glimpse as we grow to eight figures this year.

What You'll Do

 - Own the full sales cycle from prospecting to close — qualifying inbound leads, running sharp discovery, multi-threading deals when needed, and closing at pace across a high-volume book of business.

 - Drive net new revenue by targeting CPG brands and engaging key stakeholders across finance, accounting, and operations.

 - Apply a structured, repeatable sales methodology — leveraging MEDDPICC and Challenger principles to move deals forward with confidence and precision at every stage.

 - Maintain rigorous pipeline hygiene — accurately forecast your book, surface deal risk early, and keep opportunities moving with urgency.

 - Build deep product and market expertise to become a trusted resource for prospects navigating deductions, trade management, and revenue recovery workflows.

 - Contribute to our evolving playbook — refining messaging, email sequences, cold call strategies, and objection handling as we scale.

 - Collaborate across client success, sales development, marketing, and growth to target accounts strategically and close with full context.

 - Serve as a feedback loop between the field and product — surfacing objection patterns, competitive dynamics, and feature gaps to sharpen our offering.

Ideal Qualifications

 - 3+ years of B2B and/or SaaS sales experience is ideal; AI-tech background is a plus

 - Proven track record of meeting and exceeding quota in a fast-paced, high-volume environment

 - Experience selling to CPG brands — food & beverage/health & beauty is a strong plus

 - Comfortable working cross-functionally with teams across different functions and skill sets

 - Thrives in ambiguity and adapts quickly in a rapidly changing environment

 - Hands-on experience with modern sales tools — HubSpot, Clay, Apollo, Fathom, or similar

 - Based in NYC, in-office 5 days, and able to travel ~20% for conferences and customer meetings

 - Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with a strong emphasis on building trust and transparency.

Why Work at Glimpse?

Glimpse is one of the fastest-growing AI companies in the consumer brand space. Since launching in April 2024, we've grown from 0 to 200+ customers entirely through founder-led sales — with a massive pipeline still ahead of us. This is a rare opportunity to join early, carry real quota, and have genuine ownership in shaping how we scale. If you perform, you'll be a cornerstone of the sales org we build around you.

 - Competitive salary with meaningful equity

 - In-person team in NYC – high ownership, fast feedback loops

 - Direct impact on a company growing at an exceptional pace

 - A front-row seat to building the operating system for CPG brands

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GLIMPSE

Glimpse is the leading AI platform for CPG brands — automating critical back-office workflows like deductions management, revenue recovery, and cash application. Since launching in April 2024, we've grown from 0 to 200+ customers, raised $52M from investors including a16z, 8VC and Y Combinator.

Our AI agents retrieve deduction data, validate charges, automate cash application, and dispute invalid claims — work that would take a full-time employee years to complete. For a $1B CPG brand, a single Glimpse agent reviewed 17,000 deductions in under 24 hours, identifying over $10M in recoverable revenue.

We're building the next-generation suite of services for consumer brands and are looking for exceptional people to help us scale.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for hungry and versatile folks to join as our sales development representatives at Glimpse. Glimpse has grown extremely fast (0→200 customers in 12 months) and we are looking for gritty individuals who can help us scale 10x. As an early hire, you will not only come in and hit milestones, but also be a part of creating foundational processes with a big growth opportunity at the company. If successful, this role could evolve into several roles including Field Sales, Account Executive, Customer Success Manager, or SDR team lead.




IDEAL QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience: 1+ years as a SDR
   
   - Proven track record of exceeding quota in past sales roles
   
   - (Preferred) B2B SaaS Experience, or Experience working for companies focused on AI applications in vertical markets.

 - Attitude: Fearless when talking to new people and a motivated self-starter, eager to consistently take on new challenges

 - Tenacious: Comfortable with on-the-phone and in-person rejection, and ability to keep following up with prospects

 - Excellent Communicator: Articulates value proposition clearly and deeply understands customer pain points

 - Work Culture: Excited to contribute to a strong culture, working together IRL in our NYC office 5 days a week
   
   - Able to travel to conferences (~30%)

 - Tech Stack: Familiarity with CRM tools like Hubspot, LinkedIn, and modern AI sales tools like Clay


JOB RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead Generation: Proactively identify and qualify new business opportunities

 - Outreach: Engage with new prospects via cold calling or LinkedIn DM’ing to convert qualified leads into meetings

 - Field Sales: Identify and convert new prospects at tradeshows and conferences, and industry social events

 - Collaborate: Work closely with Sales, Growth, and CEO to refine and execute Top of Funnel strategy

WHY JOIN GLIMPSE

You'll join a company that has found genuine product-market fit and is scaling fast — with the infrastructure, capital, and team to match. Your work will directly shape how hundreds of CPG brands run their back office. And you'll have real ownership: of your accounts, your outcomes, and the AM function itself as we build it from the ground up.

 - Competitive salary with meaningful equity

 - In-person team in NYC – high ownership, fast feedback loops

 - Direct impact on a company growing at an exceptional pace

 - A front-row seat to building the operating system for CPG brands

[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/55b0e99b-a52d-4571-9cd6-9c2e534b955b/89c11611-c9ad-4587-90e3-9cda72d6cf03/Screenshot%202026-05-04%20at%201.33.48%E2%80%AFPM.png]

GLIMPSE is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. GLIMPSE will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, disability, veteran status or any other characteristic protected by law.

source_jobs.title: GTM Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GLIMPSE

Glimpse helps leading consumer brands unlock retail cashflow by building AI agents to automate critical finance workflows like revenue recovery, accounting reconciliation, and trade spend management. We work with 200+ food/beverage/health/beauty brands including IQ Bar, Sauz, Bero, and more.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a high-agency GTM Engineer to own and scale the systems that power Glimpse's outbound engine. You'll build the automation infrastructure, data pipelines, and AI-powered workflows that enable our team to move faster, experiment continuously, and convert pipeline efficiently as we scale to eight figures this year.

This role sits at the intersection of revenue operations, growth engineering, and pipeline generation. You'll own the technical execution of our GTM motion - from programmatic outbound to revenue system optimization to experiment execution - and be responsible for designing and hardening a repeatable outbound engine.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Own Revenue Operations & GTM Systems

 - Manage and optimize our GTM tech stack (HubSpot, Apollo, Clay to start)

 - Build and maintain data pipelines between CRM, sales tools, and analytics platforms to enable real-time reporting and multi-attribution

 - Design lead workflows that improve conversion rates across the funnel

 - Ensure data quality, hygiene, and observability across all GTM systems

Build Programmatic Outbound Infrastructure

 - Design and implement AI-powered outbound sequences that personalize at scale

 - Own email deliverability infrastructure including domain health, warming strategies, and sender reputation monitoring

 - Build automated research and trigger workflows using tools like Clay to improve targeting and personalization

 - Test and optimize outbound platforms to ensure consistent pipeline generation

Drive Growth Through Experimentation

 - Ship high-velocity experiments: website widgets, landing page variants, email sequences, etc.

 - Build internal tools that enable marketing and sales to launch tests without engineering support

 - Instrument conversion funnels with proper tracking and analytics to measure what's working

 - Proactively identify bottlenecks and friction points in the GTM motion and automate resilient solutions

Scale Through AI & Automation

 - Build LLM-powered workflows for prospect research, personalization, content generation, and lead qualification

 - Automate manual processes across marketing, sales, and operations to reduce drag on revenue teams

 - Create scalable systems that compound over time, not one-off scripts or hacks




WHO YOU ARE

Required:

 - 2+ years in GTM, RevOps, or Growth engineering roles at B2B SaaS companies

 - Hands-on experience with HubSpot and Apollo (or similar outbound automation platforms)

 - Strong technical skills: comfortable with APIs, webhooks, light scripting (Python/JavaScript), and SQL

 - Comfortable vibe coding and prototyping quickly

 - Track record of rapid experimentation and shipping growth initiatives that drive measurable results

 - High agency and bias toward action - you see a problem and fix it rather than wait for direction

Bonus:

 - Experience building automated outbound systems and understanding email deliverability best practices

 - Familiarity with Hubspot, Clay, Apollo, or other modern growth tools

 - Background in CPG, Retail, or vertical SaaS




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - Pipeline generation increases measurably through outbound systems you build and optimize

 - GTM teams spend less time on prospecting and more time on selling

 - Experiments ship weekly, not monthly

 - Data flows reliably between systems and powers real-time observability

 - You proactively identify opportunities for automation and deliver solutions end-to-end

WHY JOIN GLIMPSE

You'll join a company that has found genuine product-market fit and is scaling fast — with the infrastructure, capital, and team to match. Your work will directly shape how

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GLIMPSE

Glimpse is the leading AI platform for CPG brands — automating critical back-office workflows like deductions management, revenue recovery, and cash application. Since launching in April 2024, we've grown from 0 to 200+ customers, raised $52M from investors including a16z, 8VC and Y Combinator.

Our AI agents retrieve deduction data, validate charges, automate cash application, and dispute invalid claims — work that would take a full-time employee years to complete. For a $1B CPG brand, a single Glimpse agent reviewed 17,000 deductions in under 24 hours, identifying over $10M in recoverable revenue.

We're building the next-generation suite of services for consumer brands and are looking for exceptional people to help us scale.

ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a talented and driven full-stack engineer with 3+ years of experience to join our dynamic team. This role requires a highly skilled individual comfortable with rapid development cycles and end-to-end project ownership.


RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Develop and maintain end-to-end front-end and back-end applications.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design and implement new features.

 - Push complete projects into production from start to finish.

 - Participate in code reviews and contribute to best practices.

 - Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues.

 - Ensure the technical feasibility of UI/UX designs.


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

 - Minimum 3 years in full-stack development.

 - Exceptional coding skills and ability to ship code quickly.

 - Proficient in Python, Node.js, Next.js

 - Experience building with LLMs.

 - Strong problem-solving abilities and comfortable working in a fast-paced environment.

 - Excellent communication skills and a team-oriented attitude.

 - Ability to work from our NY office full-time.


TECHNOLOGIES:

 - Python, Node.js, Next.js

 - MongoDB, PostgreSQL


WHY JOIN GLIMPSE

You'll join a company that has found genuine product-market fit and is scaling fast — with the infrastructure, capital, and team to match. Your work will directly shape how hundreds of CPG brands run their back office. And you'll have real ownership: of your accounts, your outcomes, and the AM function itself as we build it from the ground up.

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
About Glimpse

Glimpse is the leading AI platform for CPG brands — automating critical back-office workflows like deductions management, revenue recovery, and cash application. Since launching in April 2024, we've grown from 0 to 200+ customers, raised $52M from investors including a16z, 8VC and Y Combinator.

Our AI agents retrieve deduction data, validate charges, automate cash application, and dispute invalid claims — work that would take a full-time employee years to complete. For a $1B CPG brand, a single Glimpse agent reviewed 17,000 deductions in under 24 hours, identifying over $10M in recoverable revenue.

We're building the next-generation suite of services for consumer brands and are looking for exceptional people to help us scale.

About the Role

Glimpse is looking for an Enterprise Account Executive to own new business growth with our largest and most strategic accounts. Reporting to the VP of Sales, you'll be responsible for driving net new revenue by identifying, engaging, and closing complex deals with major food, beverage, health, and beauty brands across the US. You'll be expected to meet and exceed quota by running a disciplined, multi-stakeholder sales motion — and play a defining role in building Glimpse's enterprise business from the ground up as we scale to eight figures this year.

What You'll Do

 - Own the full enterprise sales cycle — from executive-level discovery through complex negotiations, procurement, and close — on a smaller number of higher-value, longer-horizon deals.

 - Drive net new revenue by identifying and engaging strategic prospects within major CPG brands, building relationships across finance, accounting, operations, and C-suite stakeholders.

 - Lead multi-threaded deals with discipline — managing multiple stakeholders, navigating procurement and legal processes, and maintaining momentum across extended sales cycles.

 - Develop and execute account plans that map Glimpse's value to each prospect's specific financial workflows, business priorities, and organizational structure.

 - Apply a structured enterprise sales methodology — leveraging MEDDPICC/ Challenger and executive selling principles to qualify rigorously and advance deals with precision.

 - Partner with sales leadership and product to define enterprise structure, pricing, and positioning — and feed market intelligence back into our roadmap and GTM strategy.

 - Build and codify the enterprise sales playbook, establishing the frameworks, processes, and benchmarks that will define how Glimpse scales upmarket.

 - Represent Glimpse at industry events and conferences, building pipeline and elevating our presence with the brands and buyers that matter most.

Ideal Qualifications

 - 6+ years of enterprise B2B sales experience, ideally in SaaS, fintech, or AI-enabled services

 - Proven track record of closing six-figure and seven-figure deals with complex, multi-stakeholder buying processes

 - Experience engaging and building trust at the CFO, VP Finance, or Controller level

 - Skilled at navigating enterprise procurement, legal, and security review cycles

 - Experience selling to CPG brands

 - Hands-on experience with modern sales tools — HubSpot, Clay, Apollo, Fathom, or similar

 - Comfortable operating in an early-stage environment where the infrastructure is being built as we go

 - Based in NYC and able to travel ~30% for customer meetings, on-sites, and conferences

 - Exceptional communicator — compelling in the boardroom, precise in writing, and credible at every level of a buying organization

Why Work at Glimpse?

Enterprise is a greenfield opportunity at Glimpse. We've proven product-market fit at mid-market scale — and the largest brands in our space represent our biggest untapped opportunity. As an Enterprise AE, you'll help define the motion, have the support of founders who are deeply invested in your success, and the chance to own a segment that can meaningfully change the trajectory of the company.

source_jobs.title: Director of Growth
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GLIMPSE

Glimpse is the leading AI platform for CPG brands — automating critical back-office workflows like deductions management, revenue recovery, and cash application. Since launching in April 2024, we've grown from 0 to 200+ customers and raised $52M from investors including a16z, 8VC and Y Combinator.

Our AI agents retrieve deduction data, validate charges, automate cash application, and dispute invalid claims — work that would take a full-time employee years to complete. For a $1B CPG brand, a single Glimpse agent reviewed 17,000 deductions in under 24 hours, identifying over $10M in recoverable revenue.

We're building the next-generation suite of services for consumer brands and are looking for exceptional people to help us scale.

ABOUT THE ROLE

Build and scale a repeatable, data-driven growth engine that drives pipeline and revenue across self-serve and sales-assisted channels, while establishing the foundational GTM systems, team, and positioning needed to take Glimpse from ~200 customers to category leadership in retail AI.

This role is less about “brand CMO” and more about owning growth + GTM architecture end-to-end—a critical hire for scaling post–product-market fit .

WHAT YOU'LL DO

1. Scale a Data-Driven Demand Generation Function

 - Events: Take a functioning events program and manage it — expanding format, frequency, and geographic reach while maintaining strong pipeline ROI.

 - Product Marketing: Own core messaging, positioning, and sales enablement. Translate complex back-office workflows into a sharp ROI narrative (recovering 3%+ of topline) that compresses sales cycles and improves win rates against the status quo.

 - Content: Scale a content engine built on CPG-specific research, deduction benchmarks, and customer case studies — driving organic inbound pipeline and cementing Glimpse as the definitive voice on retail revenue leakage.

 - Implement and maintain full-funnel GTM infrastructure — attribution modeling, CRM hygiene, funnel tracking, and campaign analytics — giving the CEO, Sales, and CS a single source of truth on marketing's contribution to revenue.

 - Run a consistent operating cadence: weekly experiment reviews, monthly channel performance reporting, and quarterly pipeline forecasts that keep the team accountable to outcomes, not outputs.

2. Own Performance Marketing Channels End-to-End

 - Take direct ownership of all paid and performance channels — paid search, paid social, display, retargeting, and ABM — with full accountability for pipeline contribution, CAC, and payback periods.

 - Build and run a rigorous experimentation engine: design weekly A/B tests across ads, landing pages, audiences, and offers; track win rates; and systematically scale what works.

 - Architect the attribution and analytics infrastructure that gives you and the company clear, trustworthy data on channel ROI — moving spend decisions from intuition to evidence.

 - Drive outbound-assisted inbound in close partnership with Sales: define ICP targeting, build ABM plays for Fortune 500 CPG accounts, and ensure paid programs are tightly integrated with the sales motion.

 - Continuously identify and test new acquisition levers — including AI-driven tactics and emerging channels — to stay ahead of the curve.

3. Build and Lead a High-Performing Marketing Team

 - Recruit, develop, and retain a high-caliber team — including a Growth/Performance IC, Product Marketing Manager, Director of Community, and Content Associate — and set the bar for what great marketing talent looks like at Glimpse.

 - Establish clear goals, ownership, and accountability for each direct report so that every team member knows what they own, what success looks like, and how their work connects to revenue.

 - Coach and develop your team actively — through structured feedback, regular 1:1s, and stretch opportunities — so the function gets stronger as it scales.

 - Operate as a player-coach early on: comfortable executing alongside

source_jobs.title: Community Associate
source_jobs.description:
About Glimpse

Glimpse is the leading AI platform for CPG brands — automating critical back-office workflows like deductions management, revenue recovery, and cash application. Since launching in April 2024, we've grown from 0 to 200+ customers, raised $52M from investors including a16z, 8VC and Y Combinator.

Our AI agents retrieve deduction data, validate charges, automate cash application, and dispute invalid claims — work that would take a full-time employee years to complete. For a $1B CPG brand, a single Glimpse agent reviewed 17,000 deductions in under 24 hours, identifying over $10M in recoverable revenue.

We're building the next-generation suite of services for consumer brands and are looking for exceptional people to help us scale.

About the Role

As Community Associate, you'll sit at the center of Glimpse's community and events engine — reporting directly to the Director of Community. As the Director moves into a more strategic, enterprise-focused role, you'll be the operational core that keeps everything running and growing.

You'll own two tracks simultaneously: (1) building out the ops and logistics infrastructure needed to support our enterprise-level community program, and (2) independently standing up and scaling community and events for our mid-market and SMB segment (brands at $100M and under in retail revenue). This is a high-ownership, high-visibility role — you'll be in the room, behind the scenes, and everything in between.

What You'll Do

 - Events & Logistics
   
   - Own end-to-end execution of mid-market and SMB events: venue sourcing, vendor coordination, maintaining budgets, RSVPs, run-of-show, and day-of logistics
   
   - Support the Director of Community on enterprise event execution — including high-touch dinners, partner activations, and hosted experiences with anchor sponsors
   
   - Manage event timelines and ensure nothing falls through the cracks across simultaneous events in multiple cities
   
   - Travel as needed to host, connect with, and build relationships with attendees in person — serving as a Glimpse ambassador on the ground
   
   
   COMMUNITY OPS & CRM
   
   - Formalize and build out Glimpse's community operations infrastructure — including CRM setup, contact management, and event tracking workflows (HubSpot experience a plus)
   
   - Own day-to-day community operations: member communications, onboarding, follow-ups, and relationship maintenance across the mid-market and SMB segment
   
   - Build repeatable processes and playbooks for how we run, track, and measure community and events
   
   - Track pipeline attribution and report on events performance to the Director of Community and broader GTM team
   
   
   PIPELINE & OUTREACH
   
   - Drive event RSVPs and qualified attendance through proactive outbound — cold email, LinkedIn outreach, warm intros, and conference targeting
   
   - Build and maintain relationships with prospects, connectors, and partners to grow event audiences over time
   
   - Coordinate with co-hosts and partners on joint events to extend reach and co-market across the CPG ecosystem
   
   - Treat community and events as a sales channel — you understand pipeline and are motivated by seeing community turn into revenue
   
   
   CONTENT & COMMUNITY PRESENCE
   
   - Support the content layer around events and community — recap posts, LinkedIn content, email follow-ups, and community newsletters
   
   - Help develop Glimpse's presence within the CPG finance and operations community by surfacing relevant events, associations, and conversations
   
   
   WHO YOU ARE
   
   - 2–4 years of experience in community, events, field marketing, partnerships, or BD — ideally at a B2B startup or high-growth company
   
   - Operator first. You're diligent, executional, and ownership-driven — you run things to completion and don't need to be told twice
   
   - Highly organized project manager. You're juggling multiple events at once, managing vendors and

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT GLIMPSE

Glimpse is the leading AI platform for CPG brands — automating critical back-office workflows like deductions management, revenue recovery, and cash application. Since launching in April 2024, we've grown from 0 to 200+ customers, raised $52M from investors including a16z, 8VC and Y Combinator.

Our AI agents retrieve deduction data, validate charges, automate cash application, and dispute invalid claims — work that would take a full-time employee years to complete. For a $1B CPG brand, a single Glimpse agent reviewed 17,000 deductions in under 24 hours, identifying over $10M in recoverable revenue.

We're building the next-generation suite of services for consumer brands and are looking for exceptional people to help us scale.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Customer Success Engineer at Glimpse, you own the success of our mid-market and enterprise customers. That means getting hands-on: project managing internal stakeholders to unblock data integrations, coordinating with customers to gather the materials needed for our disputing agents, and doing whatever it takes to make each customer successful on the platform.

 

You'll also be constantly improving the customer experience - identifying what's manual, what's breaking, and what should work better. This is an IC role that rewards people who thrive in ambiguity, move fast, and take pride in turning messy operational problems into clean solutions.

 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

· Own the post-sale experience for mid-market and enterprise customers, driving fast time-to-value and keeping customers active, healthy, and seeing results

· Assist with complex, multi-stakeholder onboardings, coordinating across internal teams and customer contacts to resolve data integration bottlenecks

· Proactively reach out to customers to gather context and materials needed to run disputing workflows effectively

· Monitor customer health and adoption, surface risks early, and intervene before they become issues

· Own inbound support and escalations, responding with speed and clarity and knowing when to pull in other teams

· Identify manual components of the role and build systems to automate or streamline them over time

· Translate customer feedback into clear product input and serve as the voice of the customer with our engineering and product teams

· Partner with Account Managers on renewal and expansion conversations using adoption and health data

 


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

The three things that matter most

· Strong bias to action. You see a problem and move. You don't wait for a perfect playbook, you write one.

· Operational and technical fluency. You're comfortable navigating complex products, data flows, and integrations, and you can troubleshoot without escalating everything.

· Strong communication and customer presence. You write clearly, handle tough conversations with grace, and can hold a room whether you're talking to a CFO or an accountant.

Background

· 2–6 years in a technical customer success or solutions role, or in a high-slope field such as consulting, banking, early-stage startups, and finance/operations-heavy software companies

· Direct experience working with customers, ideally in enterprise or complex B2B environments

· High agency: you've operated in ambiguous, fast-moving environments and figured things out without a manual

· You’ve worked to deliver or support customers with a technical product

· (Preferred) Exposure to AI workflow products, finance, accounting, or CPG software

· (Preferred) Experience at an early-stage tech company where the environment was constantly evolving

· (Preferred) Engineering, CS, or technical degree




WHY JOIN GLIMPSE

You'll join a company that has found genuine product-market fit and is scaling fast — with the infrastructure, capital, and team to match. Your work will directly shape how hundreds of CPG brands run their back office. And you'll have real ownership: of your accounts, your outcomes, and the AM function itse

notes: Glimpse is a rapidly growing AI platform for CPG brands, with a strong focus on sales and customer success. While they have a significant number of tech roles, the majority of their job postings are for sales and business development positions.
